dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4510 IS: Relief for Families of the Fallen Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Ms. Hassan (for herself and Mr. Cornyn) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance

form.official-title: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to provide tax relief to the families of public safety officers who died as a result of injuries sustained in the line of duty.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Relief for Families of the Fallen Act.

section.header: Tax relief for families of public safety officers who died as a result of injuries sustained in the line of duty

subsection.text: Section 692 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended—

paragraph.text: in the heading, by inserting public safety officers, after astronauts,, and
by adding at the end the following new subsection:

subsection.header: Public safety officers dying as a result of injuries sustained in the line of duty

paragraph.text: In the case of any public safety officer who dies as the direct and proximate result of a personal injury sustained in the line of duty, any tax imposed by this subtitle shall not apply—

subparagraph.text: with respect to the taxable year in which falls the date of the death of such public safety officer, and
with respect to any prior taxable year in the period beginning with the last taxable year ending before the taxable year in which the relevant personal injury was sustained in the line of duty.

For purposes of determining whether a public safety officer died as the direct and proximate result of a personal injury sustained in the line of duty, the Secretary shall, to the extent practicable, use the same criteria used when determining whether a public safety officer died as the direct and proximate result of a personal injury sustained in the line of duty for purposes of payment of a benefit under section 1201(a) of title I of the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 (34 U.S.C. 10281(a)).
For purposes of making determinations described in subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall ensure that such determinations are—

clause.text: made as expeditiously as possible, and

to the maximum extent practicable, made in a manner which minimizes any administrative burdens placed on the family members or legal representatives of deceased public safety officers who have made a claim for the application of paragraph (1) with respect to any taxable year.

subparagraph.text: Nothing in this subsection shall be construed to require that any determination be made with respect to payment of a benefit under section 1201(a) of title I of the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 (34 U.S.C. 10281(a)) for purposes of the application of this subsection.

paragraph.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term public safety officer has the same meaning given such term under section 1206 of the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 (34 U.S.C. 10284).
Section 5(b)(1) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended by inserting public safety officers, after astronauts,.
Section 6013(f)(2)(B) of such Code is amended by inserting public safety officers, after astronauts,.

subsection.text: The item in the table of sections for part II of subchapter J of chapter 1 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 relating to section 692 is amended to read as follows:

toc.toc-entry: Sec. 692. Income taxes of members of Armed Forces, astronauts, public safety officers, and victims of certain terrorist attacks on death.

subsection.text: The amendments made by this section shall apply with respect to any public safety officer (as defined in section 692(e)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as added by this section) whose death occurs on or after January 1, 2025.
